title: Build documentation in a container on Win arm64
body:
Locally building the Windows installer requires building the documentation. And building documentation requires Pandoc.

There are no pre-built binaries for Windows arm64, which makes it complicated to build the Podman Windows installer on it.

To unlock this scenario, we are adding a new `winmake.ps1` target to build the documentation in a container (where Pandoc is pre-installed).

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1cc1d414-24e4-4273-8103-d7ad23a044b8)



#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

comments.body: A couple of nitty user facing typos that should be cleaned, otherwise LGTM

> I don't mind this change but it does raise the question to me what is the point of using pandoc to generate html files for the windows installer? Does anyone actually use the shipped files from the windows installer? (I guess we cannot really know)
How would a user access the file son windows?

It's quite visible because it's shown to users if they press the (selected-by-default) button at the end of the installation. 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dbfac00c-8fcf-4c34-bbba-34ec750c86dd)

i  do like the idea of using something other than pandoc for creating our docs.  Ideally it would be something that works everywhere.  But I am also OK merging this as an incremental step bc it is better than what we have today.

LGTM
